---
title: "ANOVA 3 & 4"
format: 
  clean-typst:
    bibliographystyle: "apa"
    include-in-header: "custom.typ"
    commands: [small-cite]
execute: 
  echo: false
  warning: false    
author:
  - name: A Pathmeswaran
    orcid: 0000-0003-4065-2639
    email: pathmes@kln.ac.lk
date: 2026-02-25
date-format: long
bibliography: [static/references.bib]
handout: false
brand:
  typography: 
    fonts: 
      - family: Roboto
        weight: [100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700]
        style: [normal, italic]
        source: google
    base: Roboto
    headings: Roboto
---

### Syllabus

* Factorial ANOVA
    + Main effects & interaction
    + Calculation
    + Interpretation of two way ANOVA table
    
* Completely randomized design
* Randomised block design
* Balance incomplete block design,
* Latin square design,
* Repeated measure ANOVA, MANOVA, ANCOVA



```{r}
#|label: load_packages
#|echo: false
#|message: false
#|warning: false

suppressMessages(library(tidyverse))
suppressMessages(library(gtsummary))
suppressMessages(library(broom))
suppressMessages(library(car))
suppressMessages(library(MASS))
suppressMessages(library(tibble))
suppressMessages(library(pixiedust))
suppressMessages(library(tinytable))
suppressMessages(library(tinyplot))
```
### Tooth Growth Data

A data set with 60 observations on 3 variables (sample data in R)  

**len**		Tooth length  
**supp**	Supplement type (VC or OJ)  
**dose**	Dose in milligrams/day  


### Explore the data
```{r}
#| message: false
df2 <- as_tibble(ToothGrowth)
df2$dose <- factor(df2$dose,
                   levels = c(0.5, 1, 2),
                   labels = c("D0.5", "D1.0", "D2.0"))

set.seed(1234)
df2 %>% 
  sample_n(10)  |> 
  tt()
```

### Data Summary

```{r}
#| message: false


df2 %>% 
  tbl_summary(statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                    digits = list(len ~ c(1, 2))) 
```



### Cross tabulation of supp and dose.
```{r message=FALSE}
tbl_cross(
  df2,
  row = dose,
  col = supp,
  margin = c("column", "row"),
  margin_text = "Total"
)
```
  
  
### Summarize length by supp  

```{r message=FALSE}
df2 %>% 
  group_by(supp) %>% 
  summarise(
    count = n(),
    Mean = mean(len),
    SD = sd(len)
  )  |> 
  tt() |> 
  format_tt(j = 3:4, fn = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)) 
  
```

### Summarize length by dose

```{r}
df2 %>% 
  group_by(dose) %>%
  summarise(    count = n(),
                Mean = mean(len),
                SD = sd(len)
  ) |> 
  tt() |> 
  format_tt(j = 3:4, fn = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)) 
```


### Summarize length by supp and dose

```{r}
#| warning: false
#| message: false
df2 %>% 
  group_by(supp, dose) %>%
  summarise(    count = n(),
                Mean = mean(len),
                SD = sd(len)
  ) |> 
  tt() |> 
  format_tt(j = 4:5, fn = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)) 
```

### Boxplots

:::: {layout-ncol=2}
::: {#first-column}
```{r}

tinyplot(len ~ supp | dose, data = df2, type = "boxplot")
```
:::

::: {#second-column}
```{r}
tinyplot(len ~ dose | supp, data = df2, type = "boxplot")

```
:::
:::::




### Boxplot
```{r}
#| label: bp1
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 6
 
tinyplot(len ~ dose | supp, data = df2, type = "boxplot")

```

### Two way ANOVA - No Interaction


```{r}
#| message: false
r_aov2 <- aov(len ~ supp + dose, data = df2)
tidy(anova(r_aov2)) %>% 
  add_row(term = "Total", df = sum(.$df), sumsq = sum(round(.$sumsq)) ) %>% 
  dust() |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 3:4, round = 1) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 5, round = 2) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 6, fn = quote(pval_string(value))) |>
  sprinkle_colnames(term = "Term",
                    df = "DF",
                    sumsq = "SSq",
                    meansq = "MSq",
                    statistic = "VR",
                    p.value = "P-value") |> 
 knitr::kable(align = c('l', 'r', 'r', 'r', 'r', 'r'))
```


This is the results of an additive model.  The effect of the two factors on tooth growth is independent of each other.    
We could check and see if there is an interaction between the two factors - the effect of one factor is altered by the level of the other factor.   


### Model Summary  

```{r}
model.tables(r_aov2, type = "means", se = TRUE)

```

### Post hoc comparisons  
#### Tukey multiple comparisons of means 95% family-wise confidence level
```{r}
hsd1 <- TukeyHSD(r_aov2) 
hsd1
```

### Check homogeneity of Variance

```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 5
plot(r_aov2, 1)
```

### Check Normality

```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 5
plot(r_aov2, 2)

```
$$\phi(x_i) = \frac{(x_i - 0.5)}{n}$$





### Formal test for Normality

This can be formally tested by using the Shapiro-Wilk test  

```{r comment=""}
r_aov2 %>%
residuals() %>%
shapiro.test()
```

### Two way ANOVA - With Interaction

We could check and see if there is an interaction between the two factors - the effect of one factor is altered by the level of the other factor.   

```{r message=FALSE}
r_aov2i <- aov(len ~ supp * dose, data = df2)
tidy(anova(r_aov2i)) %>% 
  add_row(term = "Total", df = sum(.$df), sumsq = sum(round(.$sumsq)) ) %>% 
    dust() |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 3:4, round = 1) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 5, round = 2) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 6, fn = quote(pval_string(value))) |>
  sprinkle_colnames(term = "Term",
                    df = "DF",
                    sumsq = "SSq",
                    meansq = "MSq",
                    statistic = "VR",
                    p.value = "P-value") |> 
  knitr::kable()
  
```
 
### Model Summary   

```{r}
model.tables(r_aov2i, type = "means", se = TRUE)[1]
model.tables(r_aov2i, type = "means", se = TRUE)[3]
```


### Post hoc comparisons  

```{r}
hsd2 <- TukeyHSD(r_aov2i)
```

```{r}
x <- as_tibble(
  as.data.frame(hsd2$dose, 
                row.names = names(hsd1$dose[,1])), rownames = NA)
x <- rownames_to_column(x, "Groups")
tt(x) |>   format_tt(j = c(2:4), fn = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)) |> 
  format_tt(j = c(5), fn = function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x)) |> 
  style_tt(j = c(2:5), align = 'r')

y <- as_tibble(
  as.data.frame(hsd2$supp, 
                row.names = names(hsd1$supp[,1])), rownames = NA)
y <- rownames_to_column(y, "Groups")

tt(y) |>   format_tt(j = c(2:4), fn = function(x) sprintf("%.1f", x)) |> 
  format_tt(j = c(5), fn = function(x) sprintf("%.3f", x)) |> 
  style_tt(j = c(2:5), align = 'r')

```



### Check homogeneity of Variance

```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
plot(r_aov2i, 1)
```

### Check Normality

```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
plot(r_aov2i, 2)

```

### Formal test for Normality


```{r}
r_aov2i %>%
residuals() %>%
shapiro.test()
```

### ANCOVA

Pre and Post treatment weight of patients with anorexia.  The treatment groups were Cont = Control, CBT = Cognitive Behavior Therapy & FT = Family Therapy (Source: the data set is in the MASS package)


### Sample data
```{r message=FALSE}
df1 <- anorexia
df1$Treat <- factor(df1$Treat, levels = c("Cont", "CBT", "FT"))
set.seed(1234)
sample_n(df1, 10) %>% 
  knitr::kable()
```

### Data Summary

```{r}
#| message: false
df1 %>% tbl_summary(statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                    digits = list(c(Prewt, Postwt) ~ c(1, 2)))
```


### Data Summary

```{r}
#| message: false
df1 %>% tbl_summary(by = Treat,
                    statistic = all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                    digits = list(c(Prewt, Postwt) ~ c(1, 2)))
```


### Visualize - scatterplot

```{r}
ggplot(data = df1, mapping = aes(x = Prewt, y = Postwt)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.67) 

```

### Visualize - scatterplot by treatment

```{r}

ggplot(data = df1, mapping = aes(x = Prewt, y = Postwt, color = Treat)) + 
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.67) 

```

### ANCOVA without interaction (main effects only)
```{r message=FALSE}
r_aov3 <- aov(Postwt ~ Treat + Prewt, data = df1)
tidy(anova(r_aov3)) %>% 
  add_row(term = "Total", df = sum(.$df), sumsq = sum(round(.$sumsq)) ) %>% 
    dust() |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 3:4, round = 1) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 5, round = 2) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 6, fn = quote(pval_string(value))) |>
  sprinkle_colnames(term = "Term",
                    df = "DF",
                    sumsq = "SSq",
                    meansq = "MSq",
                    statistic = "VR",
                    p.value = "P-value") |> 
  knitr::kable()
```

### Post hoc comparisons 
```{r comment="", warning=FALSE}

TukeyHSD(r_aov3, "Treat")
```

### Homogeneity of variance
```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
plot(r_aov3, 1)

```

### Normality plot of the residuals
```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
plot(r_aov3, 2)
```

### Shapiro-Wilk test
```{r comment=""}
r_aov3 %>%
residuals() %>%
shapiro.test()
```

### ANCOVA with interaction
```{r message=FALSE}
r_aov3i <- aov(Postwt ~ Treat * Prewt, data = df1)
tidy(anova(r_aov3i)) %>% 
  add_row(term = "Total", df = sum(.$df), sumsq = sum(round(.$sumsq)) ) %>% 
    dust() |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 3:4, round = 1) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 5, round = 2) |> 
  sprinkle(cols = 6, fn = quote(pval_string(value))) |>
  sprinkle_colnames(term = "Term",
                    df = "DF",
                    sumsq = "SSq",
                    meansq = "MSq",
                    statistic = "VR",
                    p.value = "P-value") |> 
    knitr::kable()
```
### Post hoc comparisons 
```{r comment="", warning=FALSE}

TukeyHSD(r_aov3i, "Treat")
```


### Homogeneity of Variance

```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
plot(r_aov3i, 1)
```

### Normality plot of the residuals
```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
plot(r_aov3i, 2)
```

### Shapiro-Wilk test
```{r comment=""}
r_aov3i %>%
residuals() %>%
shapiro.test()
```

### Plot of the raw data and fitted values

```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 6
#| message: false
df1$fit3 <- fitted(r_aov3)


ggplot(data = df1, aes(x = Prewt, y = Postwt, color = Treat) ) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.67) +
geom_line(aes(y = fit3), size = 1)


```


### Plot of the raw data and fitted values
#### With interaction
```{r}
#|fig-width: 12
#|fig-height: 5
#| message: false
df1$fit3i <- fitted(r_aov3i)



ggplot(data = df1, aes(x = Prewt, y = Postwt, color = Treat) ) +
geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.67) +
geom_line(aes(y = fit3i), size = 1)

```
